purpose: Defines release gates for standalone hardening builds with adversarial validation, explicit active-versus-lineage separation, and frozen-snapshot regeneration requirements.
gates: All active package metadata must identify the build as current_release.
Manifest counts, validation counts, provenance counts, and filesystem counts must be compared in a gold-master consistency manifest.
Older version tokens are permitted only in explicit lineage-retained, reference-history, chronology, legacy, or provenance zones.
Active validation artifacts must be regenerated from one frozen final filesystem snapshot.
Truth-layer overreach checks must flag any enriched expression mislabeled as direct canon.
Critical object classes must meet saturation contracts before release.
Runtime-ready branches must emit validated world effects and world deltas or remain explicitly candidate-only.
Cross-world system layers for magic, language, infrastructure, and conflict must exist as standalone baseline files.
fail_if: active files contain stale version tokens
distribution root does not match package manifest
zip package is incomplete or not standalone
active validation is not regenerated from the frozen snapshot
governance policy and package state disagree
critical object saturation gates are unmet
usable runtime branches lack validated world deltas
